title: Services, Units, Boot State, and Logs · Command Brief: Unit Configuration
summary: Diagnose a service that is enabled but unavailable by separating desired boot state, current process state, dependencies, configuration, and logs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux unit configuration increment in a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect vendor units, drop-ins, environment sources, daemon reload requirements, and effective configuration.
Query journal fields, units, boots, priorities, time ranges, cursors, and follow mode without losing causal context.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Unit Configuration
steps.instruction:
theory case — lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 4. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Unit Configuration contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect vendor units, drop-ins, environment sources, daemon reload requirements, and effective configuration.

content.text: Inspect vendor units, drop-ins, environment sources, daemon reload requirements, and effective configuration. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 4. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback. A defensible solution applies Unit Configuration, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet.

content.code:
systemctl show lab-worker.service -p ActiveState -p SubState -p Result
journalctl -u lab-worker.service -b --since '10 minutes ago'
# Restart only after evidence identifies a reversible cause.
content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Editing a vendor unit in place is the safest persistent customization. This fails the Unit Configuration contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Defend the Unit Configuration model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Unit Configuration: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — lab-worker.service is enabled and active, but readiness failed after configuration revision 5. Query effective unit settings and journal events from this boot before any restart or rollback.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inspect vendor units, drop-ins, environment sources, daemon reload requirements, and effective configuration.
